stomach.en: the organ inside your body where food is digested

ex.en: He felt a pain in his stomach after eating too much.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 많이 먹은 뒤 위가 아팠다.

stomach.en: the front part of your body between your chest and your legs

ex.en: She lay on her stomach and read a book.
ex.ko: 그녀는 배를 깔고 엎드려 책을 읽었다.

ex.en: I had a bad stomachache all night.

storm.en: very bad weather with strong winds and rain

ex.en: The ship sailed into a terrible storm.
ex.ko: 그 배는 무서운 폭풍 속으로 들어갔다.

ex.en: He stormed out of the room without a word.
ex.ko: 그는 아무 말 없이 방에서 뛰쳐나갔다.

stove.en: a piece of kitchen equipment used for cooking food

ex.en: She left the soup boiling on the stove.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수프를 가스레인지 위에서 끓게 두었다.

stranger.en: a person you do not know

ex.en: Don't talk to strangers.

strategy.en: a plan for achieving something, especially over a long time

ex.en: The company needs a new marketing strategy.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 마케팅 전략이 필요하다.

strength.en: the quality of being physically or mentally strong

ex.en: He didn't have the strength to lift the box.
ex.ko: 그는 그 상자를 들 힘이 없었다.

strength.en: a good quality or ability that gives someone an advantage

ex.en: Patience is one of her greatest strengths.
ex.ko: 인내심은 그녀의 가장 큰 강점 중 하나다.

ex.en: The ball struck him on the head.

strike.en: a time when workers stop working to protest about pay or conditions

ex.en: The workers went on strike for higher wages.
ex.ko: 노동자들은 임금 인상을 요구하며 파업에 들어갔다.

strike.en: give someone a particular impression or sudden idea

ex.en: It struck me that she was hiding something.
ex.ko: 문득 그녀가 뭔가 숨기고 있다는 생각이 들었다.

strike.en: (of a disaster or illness) happen suddenly and cause harm

ex.en: A powerful earthquake struck the city at dawn.
ex.ko: 새벽에 강력한 지진이 그 도시를 덮쳤다.

string.en: thin cord used for tying things

ex.en: She tied the box with a piece of string.

string.en: a thin wire on a musical instrument that makes sound

ex.en: One of the guitar strings broke.

string.en: a series of similar things that happen one after another

ex.en: There has been a string of accidents on this road.

strongly.en: in a strong or forceful way

ex.en: I strongly recommend this book.

structure.en: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or connected

ex.en: The structure of the sentence is quite complex.

structure.en: something that has been built, such as a building or bridge

ex.en: The bridge is an impressive steel structure.

stuff.en: things in general, or a group of things (informal)

ex.en: Where should I put all this stuff?
ex.ko: 이 물건들을 다 어디에 두면 될까?

stuff.en: fill or push something into a space tightly

ex.en: He stuffed his clothes into the bag.

style.en: the particular way in which something is done

ex.en: I like her relaxed teaching style.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 편안한 교수 방식을 좋아한다.

style.en: a fashionable or attractive way of dressing or appearing

ex.en: She always dresses with great style.
ex.ko: 그녀는 언제나 아주 멋스럽게 옷을 입는다.

succeed.en: achieve what you were trying to do

ex.en: She finally succeeded in passing the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마침내 그 시험에 합격하는 데 성공했다.

succeed.en: take over a position or role from someone else

ex.en: He succeeded his father as head of the company.
ex.ko: 그는 아버지의 뒤를 이어 회사의 대표가 되었다.

success.en: the achievement of something you wanted or planned

ex.en: Hard work is the key to success.

success.en: something or someone that does very well

ex.en: The concert was a huge success.

successfully.en: in a way that achieves what was wanted or planned

ex.en: The team successfully completed the project.
I've never seen such a beautiful sunset.
ex.ko: 나는 그렇게 아름다운 노을을 본 적이 없다.

such.en: used to emphasize a large degree of something

ex.en: It was such a hot day.
There was a sudden change in the weather.

suggest.en: put forward an idea or plan for others to consider

ex.en: I suggest we leave early to avoid traffic.
ex.ko: 교통 혼잡을 피하려면 일찍 출발하자고 제안한다.

suggest.en: show that something is likely to be true

ex.en: The evidence suggests that he is innocent.

suit.en: a set of clothes made of the same material, usually a jacket with trousers or a skirt

ex.en: He wore a dark suit to the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접에 어두운색 정장을 입고 갔다.

suit.en: be right or convenient for someone, or look good on them

ex.en: That color really suits you.
ex.ko: 그 색이 너에게 정말 잘 어울려.

suit.en: a legal case brought to a court

ex.en: She filed a suit against the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 회사를 상대로 소송을 제기했다.

suitable.en: right or appropriate for a particular purpose or occasion

ex.en: This movie is not suitable for young children.

suitcase.en: a large bag with a handle for carrying clothes when travelling

ex.en: She packed her suitcase for the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행을 위해 여행 가방을 쌌다.

summary.en: a short statement giving the main points of something

ex.en: Write a short summary of the article.
ex.ko: 그 기사의 짧은 요약문을 써 보세요.

sunflower.en: a tall plant with a large yellow flower that turns toward the sun

ex.en: The field was full of bright sunflowers.

sunglasses.en: dark glasses that protect your eyes from bright sunlight

ex.en: He put on his sunglasses at the beach.

sunlight.en: the light that comes from the sun

ex.en: Sunlight poured into the kitchen.

superlative.en: the form of an adjective or adverb that expresses the highest degree

ex.en: 'Best' is the superlative of 'good'.
The chef prepared a superlative meal.

superstar.en: a very famous and successful performer or athlete

ex.en: She became a superstar overnight.

supper.en: the last meal of the day, eaten in the evening

ex.en: We had soup and bread for supper.

support.en: agree with and help someone or something

ex.en: Most people support the new plan.
ex.ko: 대부분의 사람들이 그 새 계획을 지지한다.

support.en: help, encouragement, or approval given to someone

ex.en: Thank you for all your support.

support.en: provide someone with money and the things they need to live

ex.en: He works hard to support his family.
ex.ko: 그는 가족을 부양하기 위해 열심히 일한다.

support.en: hold the weight of something so it does not fall

ex.en: These pillars support the roof.

surfing.en: the sport of riding on waves on a surfboard

ex.en: They went surfing every morning on holiday.
ex.ko: 그들은 휴가 동안 매일 아침 서핑을 하러 갔다.

surname.en: the family name shared by members of a family

ex.en: Please write your first name and surname.

surprised.en: feeling surprise because something unexpected happened

ex.en: I was surprised to see her there.
The results were quite surprising.

survive.en: continue to live, especially after a dangerous situation

ex.en: Only two passengers survived the crash.
ex.ko: 그 사고에서 승객 두 명만이 살아남았다.

survive.en: manage to get through a difficult situation

ex.en: The company barely survived the crisis.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 위기를 간신히 버텨 냈다.

swallow.en: make food or drink go down your throat

ex.en: The pill was so big he could hardly swallow it.
ex.ko: 알약이 너무 커서 그는 겨우 삼켰다.

swallow.en: a small bird with pointed wings and a forked tail

ex.en: Swallows return to the village every spring.
ex.ko: 제비들은 매년 봄 그 마을로 돌아온다.

sweat.en: the salty liquid that comes out of your skin when you are hot

ex.en: Sweat dripped down his face.

sweat.en: produce sweat, especially because you are hot or nervous

ex.en: I was sweating after the long run.

sweater.en: a warm knitted piece of clothing for the upper body

ex.en: Put on a sweater; it's cold outside.
They were childhood sweethearts.

sweetheart.en: a kind way of speaking to someone you love

ex.en: Don't cry, sweetheart.

swimming costume.en: a piece of clothing worn for swimming

ex.en: She bought a new swimming costume for the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행을 위해 새 수영복을 샀다.

swimsuit.en: a piece of clothing worn for swimming

ex.en: He forgot to pack his swimsuit.

symbol.en: something that represents an idea or quality

ex.en: The dove is a symbol of peace.

symbol.en: a sign or mark that has a particular meaning

ex.en: The symbol for water is H2O.

symphony.en: a long piece of music for a large orchestra

ex.en: They performed Beethoven's ninth symphony.

system.en: a set of connected parts or ideas that work together

ex.en: The school has a good system for tracking grades.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 성적을 관리하는 좋은 시스템을 갖추고 있다.

system.en: an organized way of doing things in society, such as government or education

ex.en: The country is reforming its health care system.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 의료 제도를 개혁하고 있다.

table tennis.en: an indoor game played on a table with small bats and a light ball

ex.en: We played table tennis after lunch.

tablespoon.en: a large spoon used for serving or measuring food

ex.en: Add two tablespoons of sugar.

talent.en: a natural ability to do something well

ex.en: She has a real talent for painting.

talent.en: people who have a natural ability to do something well

ex.en: The company is looking for young talent.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 젊은 인재를 찾고 있다.

ex.en: She tapped him on the shoulder.

tap.en: a device that controls the flow of water from a pipe

ex.en: Turn off the tap while you brush your teeth.

tap.en: a light, quick hit or its sound

ex.en: I heard a soft tap at the door.

tape.en: a long thin strip of material used for sticking or recording

ex.en: He fixed the box with sticky tape.

tape.en: stick or fasten something using tape

ex.en: She taped the poster to the wall.

tape.en: record sound or pictures on tape

ex.en: They taped the whole concert.

target.en: a result or level you are trying to reach

ex.en: We reached our sales target this month.
ex.ko: 우리는 이번 달 판매 목표를 달성했다.

target.en: an object that you aim at when shooting or attacking

ex.en: The arrow hit the center of the target.

target.en: aim something at a particular person or group

ex.en: The ads are targeted at teenagers.

task.en: a piece of work that must be done

ex.en: Cleaning the house is a big task.

tear.en: pull something apart or into pieces

ex.en: He tore the letter in half.

tear.en: a drop of salty liquid that comes from your eye

ex.en: Tears rolled down her cheeks.
She has two teenage sons.

teenager.en: a person between thirteen and nineteen years old

ex.en: The concert was full of screaming teenagers.

temperature.en: how hot or cold something or the air is

ex.en: The temperature dropped below zero last night.

temperature.en: the level of heat in your body, especially when you are ill

ex.en: The nurse took my temperature.
The loud thunder terrified the children.
ex.ko: 요란한 천둥소리가 아이들을 겁에 질리게 했다.

terrorist.en: a person who uses violence to achieve political goals

ex.en: The terrorists were arrested at the airport.

text.en: the written words in a book, article, or document

ex.en: The book has too much text and few pictures.
ex.ko: 그 책은 글이 너무 많고 그림이 적다.

text.en: a written message sent from one phone to another

ex.en: I got a text from my sister.

text.en: send a written message using a phone

ex.en: Text me when you arrive.

text message.en: a written message sent between mobile phones

ex.en: She sent me a quick text message.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나에게 짧은 문자 메시지를 보냈다.

textbook.en: a book used for studying a subject at school or college

ex.en: Open your textbooks to page ten.

theirs.en: the thing or things that belong to them

ex.en: This house is ours, and that one is theirs.
ex.ko: 이 집은 우리 것이고, 저 집은 그들의 것이다.

themselves.en: used to refer back to people or things already mentioned as the subject

ex.en: The children made breakfast themselves.

therefore.en: for that reason; as a result

ex.en: He was ill and therefore could not come.
ex.ko: 그는 아팠고, 따라서 올 수 없었다.

thief.en: a person who steals things from others

ex.en: The thief stole her purse on the bus.

third.en: coming after the second in order

ex.en: He finished in third place.

third.en: one of three equal parts of something

ex.en: A third of the students failed the test.

thirsty.en: feeling that you want to drink something

ex.en: I'm thirsty; can I have some water?
ex.ko: 목말라, 물 좀 마실 수 있을까?

thirsty.en: having a strong desire for something

ex.en: The team was thirsty for victory.
Though it was raining, we went out.

though.en: used to add a fact that makes what you said less strong

ex.en: It's hard work. I enjoy it, though.

thought.en: an idea or opinion that comes into your mind

ex.en: A sudden thought came to me.

thought.en: the process of thinking about something carefully

ex.en: She gave the plan a lot of thought.
The stadium holds ten thousand people.

thunderstorm.en: a storm with thunder, lightning, and heavy rain

ex.en: A thunderstorm knocked out the power.
Her desk is always tidy.

tidy.en: make a place neat by putting things in order

ex.en: Please tidy your room before dinner.
ex.ko: 저녁 먹기 전에 방 좀 정리해라.

tie.en: fasten or join something with a rope, string, or cord

ex.en: He tied his shoelaces.

tie.en: a narrow piece of cloth worn around the neck under a collar

ex.en: He wore a blue tie to work.
ex.ko: 그는 출근할 때 파란 넥타이를 맸다.

tie.en: a result in a game where both sides have the same score

ex.en: The match ended in a tie.

tights.en: a tight piece of clothing covering the legs and lower body

ex.en: She wore black tights under her skirt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 치마 안에 검은 타이츠를 신었다.

ex.en: The shop is open till nine.

till.en: a machine or drawer where money is kept in a shop

ex.en: She counted the money in the till.

timetable.en: a list showing the times when events or activities will happen

ex.en: Check the train timetable before you go.

tip.en: the pointed or narrow end of something

ex.en: The tip of the pencil broke.

tip.en: a small amount of money given to someone for their service

ex.en: We left a tip for the waiter.
She gave me some tips on cooking.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나에게 요리 비결을 몇 가지 알려 주었다.

tip.en: move so that one end or side goes up or down

ex.en: He tipped the bottle to pour the last drop.
ex.ko: 그는 마지막 한 방울을 따르려고 병을 기울였다.

title.en: the name of a book, film, song, or other work

ex.en: I can't remember the title of the movie.

title.en: a word such as 'Mr' or 'Doctor' used before someone's name

ex.en: What is her job title?

toast.en: bread that has been made brown and crisp by heating

ex.en: I had toast and jam for breakfast.

toast.en: an act of raising glasses and drinking to wish someone well

ex.en: Let's make a toast to the happy couple.

toe.en: one of the five parts at the end of your foot

ex.en: I stubbed my toe on the door.

tone.en: the way your voice sounds, showing your feeling or attitude

ex.en: I didn't like his angry tone.
ex.ko: 나는 그의 화난 말투가 마음에 들지 않았다.

tone.en: the general mood or character of a place, situation, or piece of writing

ex.en: The email had a friendly tone.
She painted the wall in warm tones.

tone.en: make your muscles firmer and stronger

ex.en: These exercises tone your arms.
ex.ko: 이 운동들은 팔 근육을 탄탄하게 해 준다.

ex.en: I have a terrible toothache.

toothbrush.en: a small brush used for cleaning your teeth

ex.en: Don't forget to pack your toothbrush.

tour.en: travel around a place visiting different parts of it

ex.en: They toured Europe last summer.

tour.en: a journey during which you visit several places

ex.en: We went on a guided tour of the castle.
ex.ko: 우리는 안내를 받으며 그 성을 견학했다.

tour.en: a series of performances or games in different places

ex.en: The band is on a world tour.

tour guide.en: a person whose job is to show tourists around a place

ex.en: The tour guide explained the city's history.
ex.ko: 여행 가이드가 그 도시의 역사를 설명해 주었다.

tourist.en: a person who visits a place for pleasure

ex.en: The city is full of tourists in summer.

toward.en: in the direction of someone or something

ex.en: She walked toward the door.

track.en: follow the movements of a person or thing

ex.en: The app tracks how far you run.
ex.ko: 그 앱은 네가 얼마나 달리는지 추적한다.

track.en: the pair of metal rails that a train travels on

ex.en: The train left the track after the crash.

track.en: a special path used for running or racing

ex.en: The runners lined up on the track.

track.en: one song or piece of music on an album

ex.en: My favorite track is the last one.
ex.ko: 내가 가장 좋아하는 곡은 마지막 곡이다.

trade.en: buy and sell goods, especially between countries

ex.en: The two countries trade with each other.

trade.en: the activity of buying and selling goods and services

ex.en: Trade between the countries has increased.
He traded his old bike for a skateboard.

tradition.en: a belief or way of doing things passed down over many years

ex.en: It's a family tradition to eat together on Sundays.
ex.ko: 일요일에 함께 식사하는 것은 우리 집안의 전통이다.

traditional.en: following ideas or customs that have existed for a long time

ex.en: They wore traditional costumes at the festival.

traffic.en: the vehicles moving along a road at a particular time

ex.en: There was heavy traffic on the highway.

traffic light.en: a set of colored lights that control the flow of traffic

ex.en: Stop when the traffic light turns red.

training.en: the process of learning the skills you need for an activity

ex.en: The players do fitness training every day.

training.en: the teaching you get to prepare for a particular job

ex.en: She is doing teacher training this year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 올해 교사 연수를 받고 있다.

tram.en: an electric vehicle that runs on rails along city streets

ex.en: We took the tram into the city center.

trap.en: a device or plan for catching animals or people

ex.en: They set a trap to catch the mouse.

trap.en: keep someone or something in a place they cannot escape from

ex.en: The workers were trapped inside the building.

traveler.en: a person who is travelling or who often travels

ex.en: The hotel is popular with business travelers.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 출장 여행자들에게 인기가 있다.

treasure.en: valuable things such as gold, jewels, or money

ex.en: The pirates buried their treasure on the island.

treasure.en: value something very highly and take great care of it

ex.en: She treasures the ring her grandmother gave her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니가 준 반지를 소중히 여긴다.

trick.en: deceive someone in order to get what you want

ex.en: He tricked me into giving him money.
ex.ko: 그는 나를 속여서 돈을 받아 갔다.

trick.en: a clever action meant to deceive or joke with someone

ex.en: The kids played a trick on their teacher.

trick.en: a clever or skillful act done to entertain people

ex.en: The dog can do lots of tricks.
ex.ko: 그 개는 여러 가지 재주를 부릴 수 있다.

ex.en: He is in trouble with the police.
Something is troubling her.
Thank you for going to all this trouble.

truly.en: in a true or sincere way; really

ex.en: I am truly grateful for your help.

trust.en: believe that someone is honest and will not harm you

ex.en: I trust her completely.

trust.en: a strong belief in the honesty or reliability of someone

ex.en: It takes time to build trust.

trust.en: money or property managed by one person for the benefit of another

ex.en: The money is held in trust for the children.
ex.ko: 그 돈은 아이들을 위해 신탁으로 관리된다.

truth.en: the real facts about something; what is true

ex.en: Please tell me the truth.

tune.en: a series of musical notes that make a pleasant sound

ex.en: She hummed a happy tune.

tune.en: adjust a musical instrument or machine so it works correctly

ex.en: He tuned his guitar before the show.
I've read that book twice.
ex.ko: 나는 그 책을 두 번 읽었다.

ex.en: This box is twice as heavy as that one.
ex.ko: 이 상자는 저것보다 두 배 무겁다.

ex.en: The future of the company is uncertain.
I'm uncertain about what to do next.
ex.ko: 나는 다음에 무엇을 해야 할지 확신이 서지 않는다.

uncomfortable.en: not feeling physically relaxed or at ease

ex.en: These shoes are really uncomfortable.

uncomfortable.en: feeling worried or embarrassed in a situation

ex.en: I felt uncomfortable talking about money.
ex.ko: 나는 돈 이야기를 하는 것이 거북했다.

understand.en: know the meaning of something or how it works

ex.en: I don't understand this question.

understand.en: think or believe that something is true based on what you heard

ex.en: I understand that she is leaving soon.
ex.ko: 내가 알기로 그녀는 곧 떠난다고 한다.

underwater.en: below the surface of the water

ex.en: They filmed the fish with an underwater camera.

uneasy.en: worried or slightly afraid because something may be wrong

ex.en: She felt uneasy walking home alone at night.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밤에 혼자 집에 걸어가는 것이 불안했다.

unfair.en: not treating people in an equal or right way

ex.en: It's unfair that she got all the credit.
ex.ko: 그녀가 공을 다 차지한 것은 불공평하다.

unforgettable.en: so special or striking that you cannot forget it

ex.en: The trip was an unforgettable experience.
ex.ko: 그 여행은 잊을 수 없는 경험이었다.

unfortunately.en: used to say that something is sad or disappointing

ex.en: Unfortunately, the show was cancelled.
He looked unhappy after the news.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소식을 듣고 슬퍼 보였다.

unhappy.en: not satisfied or pleased about something

ex.en: She was unhappy with her exam results.

unhealthy.en: likely to make you ill; bad for your health

ex.en: Eating too much sugar is unhealthy.
ex.ko: 설탕을 너무 많이 먹는 것은 건강에 해롭다.

ex.en: He looks pale and unhealthy.

uniform.en: a special set of clothes worn by members of a group

ex.en: All students wear a school uniform.

uniform.en: the same in all cases and at all times

ex.en: The rooms are of uniform size.

unimportant.en: not important; having little value or effect

ex.en: Don't worry about such unimportant details.
ex.ko: 그런 중요하지 않은 세부 사항은 신경 쓰지 마라.

unit.en: a fixed amount used as a standard of measurement

ex.en: The meter is a unit of length.

unit.en: a section of a book or course of study

ex.en: We are studying unit five this week.

university.en: a place of higher education where students study for degrees

ex.en: She is studying law at university.

unknown.en: not known about or not identified

ex.en: The cause of the fire is still unknown.
The film starred a young, unknown actor.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 젊은 무명 배우가 주연으로 나왔다.

ex.en: Unlike her sister, she loves sports.
The two brothers are completely unlike.
Try to avoid unnecessary spending.

unpleasant.en: not enjoyable or nice; causing bad feelings

ex.en: There was an unpleasant smell in the room.

unusual.en: not common or ordinary; different from usual

ex.en: It's unusual for him to be late.

upon.en: on; used especially in formal or written English

ex.en: The decision depends upon the results.

upset.en: unhappy or worried because something bad happened

ex.en: She was upset about losing her job.

upset.en: make someone feel unhappy or worried

ex.en: I didn't mean to upset you.

upset.en: knock something over so it turns upside down

ex.en: The cat upset the vase of flowers.

upstairs.en: to or on a higher floor of a building

ex.en: She went upstairs to bed.

upstairs.en: the part of a building above the ground floor

ex.en: The upstairs is being painted.

used.en: not new; owned or used by someone before

ex.en: They bought a used car.

used.en: familiar with something because you have done or experienced it often

ex.en: I'm used to getting up early.

useful.en: helpful for doing or achieving something

ex.en: This app is really useful for learning words.
ex.ko: 이 앱은 단어를 배우는 데 정말 유용하다.

usual.en: happening or done most of the time; normal

ex.en: We met at the usual place.

valley.en: the low land between hills or mountains, often with a river

ex.en: A river runs through the valley.

value.en: how much something is worth in money or importance

ex.en: The value of the house has gone up.

value.en: think that someone or something is important

ex.en: I value your honest advice.
ex.ko: 나는 너의 솔직한 조언을 소중히 여긴다.

value.en: the beliefs about what is right and important in life

ex.en: They share the same family values.

verb.en: a word that describes an action or a state

ex.en: 'Run' and 'be' are verbs.

video game.en: a game played on a screen using a computer or console

ex.en: The kids played video games all afternoon.
ex.ko: 아이들은 오후 내내 비디오 게임을 했다.

view.en: what you can see from a particular place

ex.en: The room has a beautiful view of the sea.

view.en: an opinion or way of thinking about something

ex.en: What are your views on the plan?
ex.ko: 그 계획에 대한 네 견해는 어때?

view.en: think about someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: Many people view him as a hero.

village.en: a small group of houses and buildings in a country area

ex.en: She grew up in a small fishing village.

violent.en: using physical force to hurt or damage

ex.en: The movie is too violent for children.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 아이들이 보기에는 너무 폭력적이다.

ex.en: A violent storm hit the coast.

violin.en: a wooden musical instrument with four strings played with a bow

ex.en: She plays the violin in an orchestra.

visitor.en: a person who visits a place or another person

ex.en: The museum gets many visitors each year.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 매년 많은 방문객이 찾는다.

vocabulary.en: all the words a person knows or that exist in a language

ex.en: Reading books helps build your vocabulary.
ex.ko: 책을 읽으면 어휘를 늘리는 데 도움이 된다.

voice.en: the sound you make when you speak or sing

ex.en: I heard a familiar voice behind me.

voice.en: the right or chance to express your opinion

ex.en: Everyone should have a voice in the decision.
ex.ko: 모두가 그 결정에 목소리를 낼 수 있어야 한다.

voice.en: express your feelings or opinions in words

ex.en: Many people voiced their concerns.

walking.en: the activity of going somewhere on foot

ex.en: Walking is good for your health.

wallet.en: a small flat case for carrying money and cards

ex.en: He took some cash out of his wallet.

wander.en: walk around slowly with no particular purpose or direction

ex.en: We wandered around the old town all day.
ex.ko: 우리는 하루 종일 구시가지를 이리저리 거닐었다.

wander.en: stop paying attention and start thinking about other things

ex.en: My mind wandered during the lecture.
ex.ko: 강의 도중 내 정신이 딴 데로 갔다.

washing machine.en: a machine that washes clothes automatically

ex.en: Put the towels in the washing machine.

washing-up.en: the job of washing the dishes after a meal

ex.en: I'll do the washing-up tonight.

weak.en: not having much physical strength or energy

ex.en: He felt weak after his illness.
ex.ko: 그는 앓고 난 뒤 몸이 약해졌다.

weak.en: not good at something, or not convincing

ex.en: Her weak point is grammar.

weak.en: containing a lot of water and little of another substance

ex.en: I don't like weak coffee.

wealth.en: a large amount of money and valuable possessions

ex.en: He gained great wealth from his business.

wealth.en: a large amount of something useful

ex.en: The website offers a wealth of information.

web.en: a net of thin threads made by a spider

ex.en: A spider spun a web in the corner.

web.en: the system of connected documents on the internet

ex.en: I found the recipe on the web.

web page.en: a single document on the internet that you can view in a browser

ex.en: Click the link to open the web page.

website.en: a set of connected web pages kept on the internet by a person or organization

ex.en: You can order tickets on our website.
ex.ko: 저희 웹사이트에서 표를 예매하실 수 있습니다.
